---
title: "About SORTEE Code Club"
title-block-banner: true

---

## Our Mission

SORTEE Code Club runs free, community-led coding sessions for researchers
in ecology and evolutionary biology. We aim to:

1. **Lower the barrier** to reproducible research practices
2. **Build community** across institutions and career stages
3. **Share knowledge** openly — all materials are CC-licensed
4. **Support SORTEE's mission** of more open, reliable, transparent research


## How Sessions Work

We meet online every third Tuesday of the month. We host two types of sessions:

- **Training Sessions**: Learn new coding skills, packages, models, and tools that support open science.
- **Hacky Hours**: Casual peer-to-peer sessions for code review, troubleshooting, and sharing tips and tricks.

[Check the full Code Club schedule](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1rOOOE7ghPduwtFftG0DJJf0DXVigAdcmQ0xdEwbKQXo/edit?gid=1314818073#gid=1314818073)

::: {.callout-tip}
## First time? Here's what to expect

Sessions are informal and collaborative. There's no such thing as a silly
question. The presenter shares their screen, you follow along, and we all
learn together.
:::

| Time | What happens |
|------|-------------|
| 0:00 | Welcome, housekeeping (5 min) |
| 0:05 | Main tutorial / demo (40 min) |
| 0:45 | Open Q&A + troubleshooting (10 min) |
| 0:55 | Wrap up, next session preview (5 min) |

Sessions are held on **Zoom**. Links are distributed via Slack
one week and again one hour before the session.


## Acknowledgements

::: {.callout-note appearance="minimal"}
SORTEE Code Club is an entirely volunteer effort. We are grateful to all
[SORTEE](https://www.sortee.org) members
who have shared their knowledge.
:::
